RESOLUTION.DESC:
[b]The Security Council,[/b]
[b]Declaring[/b] that this august body's authority lies through declarations, whether it a commendation or condemnation, a liberation or injunction, or simply a straightforward declaration,
[b]Explaining[/b] that before, nations across the multiverse have time and time again proposed proposals that this Council has put onto the books, that've recognized matters unpertaining to a singular country or region (or really any nations, for that matter),
[b]Calling back[/b] in said time to instances of the above clause when this assembly has enjoyed declarations of silly and satirical nature,
[b]Lambasting[/b] the stagnation this body faces when the world is lowering its volume and querulous debates; when most of the commendable already has been given their honors and salutes, the majority of the condemnable long been shamed and verbally defenestrated,
[b]Accepting[/b] the ability of nations to assign their most advanced or amateur authors to the Security Council to accumulate articles of creative addresses,
[b]Resorting[/b] to rejection when a piece of penning is ultimately unacceptable, not of malevolent malfeasance, but of benevolent beholding,
[b]Endorsing[/b] this assembly's ability to deny a declaration such as this declaration when it is witnessed to be undoubtedly weak and unenjoyable, and clearly crafted with breaching boredom originating from a nation's nests,
[b]Hereby declares the Security Council's somewhat positive declaration on the declarations of declarations.[/b]
RESOLUTION.NAME: Declaration on the Declarations of Declarations
